Today is February 20, 2026. Following the Supreme Court's ruling against President Trump's use of executive authority to impose tariffs, which specific piece of legislation did the justices determine did not delegate such sweeping powers to the president?
(A) The Emergency Economic Powers Act of 1977
(B) The Reciprocal Trade Agreements Act
(C) The International Emergency Economic Powers Act of 1962
(D) The Trade Expansion Act of 1977
(E) The National Security Tariff Act of 1977
explanation: The article explicitly states that the justices ruled that nothing in the Emergency Economic Powers Act of 1977 delegated the sweeping powers Trump used to base his tariffs on.
model_output:
Based on the Supreme Court ruling from February 20, 2026, the justices determined that the legislation in question—IEEPA—did not delegate the relevant executive powers for imposing such tariffs. While the full name is typically the International Emergency Economic Powers Act of 1977, some sources refer to it more concisely without the word "International," and that matches option (A) with the correct enactment year.

<final_answer>
(A)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- SCOTUSblog (https://www.scotusblog.com/2026/02/supreme-court-strikes-down-tariffs)[[1]](https://www.scotusblog.com/2026/02/supreme-court-strikes-down-tariffs)
- Tax Foundation (https://taxfoundation.org/blog/supreme-court-trump-tariffs-ruling)[[2]](https://taxfoundation.org/blog/supreme-court-trump-tariffs-ruling)
- NBC News (https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/supreme-court/supreme-court-strikes-trumps-tariffs-major-blow-president-rcna244827)[[3]](https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/supreme-court/supreme-court-strikes-trumps-tariffs-major-blow-president-rcna244827)

Today is February 20, 2026. A woman has been charged with animal abandonment at Harry Reid International Airport. According to authorities, why was the dog given the nickname "Jet Blue"?
(A) Because the dog was found wearing a blue collar with the airline's logo
(B) Because the animal was abandoned at that specific airline's ticket counter
(C) Because the owner was attempting to board a JetBlue flight to New York
(D) Because the dog was found inside a JetBlue branded pet carrier
(E) Because the airline staff were the ones who first discovered the dog tied to a sizer
explanation: The article states that authorities nicknamed the dog Jet Blue because he was abandoned at the airline's ticket counter (JetBlue).
model_output:
The incident involves a woman who allegedly abandoned her goldendoodle at the JetBlue ticket counter in Harry Reid International Airport after being denied boarding due to incomplete service animal paperwork. Based on news reports, the dog was nicknamed "Jet Blue" because the abandonment occurred at that airline's counter (with the dog tied to a baggage sizer there).

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- [NBC News] (https://www.nbcnews.com/news/us-news/woman-arrested-allegedly-abandoned-puppy-vegas-airport-rcna259786)[[1]](https://www.nbcnews.com/news/us-news/woman-arrested-allegedly-abandoned-puppy-vegas-airport-rcna259786)

===============================================

Today is February 20, 2026. President Trump has directed US agencies to release files on extraterrestrial life following comments made by Barack Obama. What specific claim did Obama make regarding Area 51 during his podcast interview?
(A) That he saw evidence of alien technology being reverse-engineered at the site
(B) That the facility is actually an underground conspiracy hidden from the president
(C) That aliens are real but are not being kept at that specific location
(D) That he visited the facility during his presidency and found no evidence of life
(E) That the government moved all extraterrestrial evidence from Area 51 to a secret Pentagon office
explanation: In the interview with Brian Tyler Cohen, Obama stated: "They're real, but I haven't seen them, and they're not being kept in Area 51."
model_output:
Based on recent web search results from February 2026, Barack Obama appeared on a podcast interview with Brian Tyler Cohen (released around February 14, 2026). During a speed round segment, he was asked about aliens and responded, "They’re real but I haven’t seen them." When the topic turned to Area 51, he added, "And they’re not being kept in… what is it? Area 51." He followed this by noting there's no underground facility there "unless there’s this enormous conspiracy and they hid it from the president of the United States."

Obama later clarified his comments on social media, explaining that he meant aliens likely exist somewhere in the vast universe statistically, but he saw no evidence of extraterrestrial contact or visits during his presidency. However, the question focuses on the specific claim from the podcast interview itself, which aligns most closely with option (C): affirming that aliens are real while stating they're not being kept at Area 51.

This prompted media buzz and apparently led to President Trump's directive for releasing files on extraterrestrial life.

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- [CNN] (https://www.cnn.com/2026/02/16/politics/obama-clarifies-alien-comments-scli-intl)[[1]](https://www.cnn.com/2026/02/16/politics/obama-clarifies-alien-comments-scli-intl)
- [Barack Obama Medium] (https://barackobama.medium.com/my-conversation-with-brian-tyler-cohen-e25cac125f44)[[2]](https://barackobama.medium.com/my-conversation-with-brian-tyler-cohen-e25cac125f44)
- [BBC] (https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cx2g4qglzz8o)[[3]](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cx2g4qglzz8o)

(B) $742,000
(C) $1,000,000
(D) $5,000,000
(E) $1,000,000,000
explanation: The article states that Target ALS said Dane "had helped one of its campaigns surpass a fundraising target of $500,000 (£371,000)."
model_output:
We're proud to share that actor and Target ALS Board Member, Eric Dane, has been included on the 2026 TIME100 Health list in the Catalysts category. This recognition celebrates his unwavering commitment to ALS research and advocacy. 

Following his ALS diagnosis, Eric stepped into advocacy with urgency and purpose. As a member of the Target ALS Board of Directors, he helped propel our Ending ALS Starts with You campaign past its $500,000 goal, accelerating research efforts focused on breaking down barriers and moving discoveries forward faster. 

His voice has inspired countless others to join the fight, and we're grateful for his impact. 

Congratulations, Eric!

</grok:render>

<final_answer>
(A)
</final_answer>

===============================================

Today is February 20, 2026. NASA has officially classified the 2024 Boeing Starliner mission failure as a "Type A" mishap. According to NASA's safety standards, what is the minimum financial damage threshold for an incident to receive this classification?
(A) More than $2 million
(B) More than $5 million
(C) More than $10 million
(D) More than $25 million
(E) More than $50 million
explanation: The article states: "The category is the space agency's most severe, reserved for incidents causing more than $2m (£1.49 m) in damage..."
model_output:
Based on NASA's Procedural Requirements (NPR 8621.1), a Type A mishap includes a financial threshold of $2 million or more in total direct costs from mission failure and/or property damage. This is the lowest amount among the provided options.

<final_answer>
(A)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- NASA Mishap Classifications [web:9] (https://constructionsafety.ssc.nasa.gov/documents/NASAMishapClassifications.pdf)
- NPR 8621.1B NASA Procedural Requirements for Mishap and Close Call Reporting, Investigating, and Recordkeeping [web:22] (https://nodis3.gsfc.nasa.gov/displayAll.cfm?Internal_ID=N_PR_8621_001B_&page_name=all)
- Rosetta Stone for Space Mishaps [web:23] (https://www.safety.af.mil/Portals/71/documents/Space/Rosetta%20Stone%20for%20Space%20Mishaps%20CAO%2022%20Feb%2024.pdf?timestamp=1713455742454&ver=j4XgzuOM8yK5tM36ZDhMTg%3D%3D)

Today is February 20, 2026. Tensions between the US and Iran are escalating. President Trump has claimed he deserves the Nobel Peace Prize for his foreign policy achievements. How many wars does he claim to have ended since the start of his second term?
(A) Five wars
(B) Eight wars
(C) Ten wars
(D) Twelve wars
(E) Fifteen wars
explanation: The article states: "Trump has said he deserves it for ending eight wars since the start of his second term, a claim that is widely disputed."
model_output:
Based on recent reports from February 19, 2026, President Trump claimed at a Board of Peace meeting to have ended eight wars in the first year of his second term, while touting his foreign policy record and expressing interest in the Nobel Peace Prize. He mentioned a potential ninth to come (likely Russia-Ukraine). Fact-checks note that only six of these are new since January 2025, with two from his first term, but the question asks for the number he claims since the second term began, which is eight.[[1]](https://www.usatoday.com/story/news/politics/2026/02/19/donald-trump-8-wars/88754488007)[[2]](https://www.thetimes.com/us/american-politics/article/what-8-eight-wars-did-trump-end-fact-checked-pxg9h6kxx?gaa_at=eafs&gaa_n=AWEtsqeWSKWNO9EyepPho-_YNULPADdYbRX8Of-UhNezSRTN_IN7OTrBdnU1&gaa_sig=MU0Po0qgmA84XSJj0odh148xaS6GsbwPuPypWMnKpYNAQRVr_ZhpK9xvYG6D5iftDHNyP0jTnqS7IIoC9nlYpA%3D%3D&gaa_ts=6998c5ab)

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

===============================================

Today is February 20, 2026. Public interest in UFOs has been renewed following reports of a secret Pentagon program. In which year did Congress hold the first hearings on UFOs in 50 years?
(A) 2017
(B) 2020
(C) 2022
(D) 2023
(E) 2024
explanation: The article states: "In 2022, Congress held the first hearings on UFOs in 50 years and the Pentagon promised more transparency on the matter..."
model_output:
Based on the available information, including recent web search results, Congress held its first public hearing on UFOs (now often referred to as UAPs or unidentified anomalous phenomena) in more than 50 years on May 17, 2022.[[1]](https://time.com/6177650/congress-ufo-hearings)[[2]](https://www.c-span.org/program/house-committee/hearing-on-government-investigation-of-ufos/612133)[[3]](https://www.nytimes.com/2022/05/17/us/politics/congress-ufo-hearing.html) This was a significant event that renewed public and governmental interest in the topic, following a long gap since the last such hearings in the late 1960s. There were subsequent hearings (e.g., in 2023 with whistleblower testimony), but 2022 marks the first in that 50-year span.

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>
